# On the user — Nikolai \Niko\ Prochazka

<!-- Slow-growing picture of the user. Only update when something non-obvious comes up. No timestamps — this is a synthesized portrait, not a log. -->

Rejects strategic voting as a standing principle, not a situational preference. Will not vote for a larger party to improve coalition odds. Treat this as a fixed constraint in any electoral or political question.

EU federalism is co-foundational — it sits at the same level as democratic values and digital rights, not below them. Not a soft preference. The user reaches for it as an identity label, not a policy checkbox.

Engages with fiscal and distributive policy philosophically. When confronted with a position (e.g., wealth tax), will reason through coherence rather than defaulting to discomfort or acceptance. Expects that kind of engagement back.
